#  | Harpy


## Getting Started

### Guides

- [ Filtering SNPs](https://pdimens.github.io/harpy/getting_started/guides/filtering_snps.md): A gentle introduction to the wild world of filtering SNPs
- [ Simulating variants](https://pdimens.github.io/harpy/getting_started/guides/simulate_variants.md): A realistic workflow to simulate variants
- [ Sort data by barcode](https://pdimens.github.io/harpy/getting_started/guides/sort_by_barcode.md): Sorting data by linked-read barcode
- [ Harpy for (non linked-read) WGS data](https://pdimens.github.io/harpy/getting_started/guides/wgs_data.md): How to use Harpy for plain-regular WGS data
- [ Choosing a software runtime method](https://pdimens.github.io/harpy/getting_started/guides/software_environments.md): Deciding between using Conda or Containers
- [ Pooling samples for SV calling](https://pdimens.github.io/harpy/getting_started/guides/sv_pooling.md): Why pool samples for SV calling and when to do it

### Resources

- [ Developing Harpy](https://pdimens.github.io/harpy/getting_started/resources/development.md): Source code structure and how to contribute to Harpy's development
- [ Running on an HPC Cluster](https://pdimens.github.io/harpy/getting_started/resources/hpc.md): Best-practices for running on a computing cluster
- [ Snakamake Things](https://pdimens.github.io/harpy/getting_started/resources/snakemake.md): Helpful things to understand about Snakemake
- [ Software used in Harpy](https://pdimens.github.io/harpy/getting_started/resources/software.md): A significant debt of gratitude; we stand on the shoulders of giants
- [ Utilities](https://pdimens.github.io/harpy/getting_started/resources/utilities.md): Utilities provided alongside a Harpy installation

### Troubleshooting

- [ Basic Troubleshooting](https://pdimens.github.io/harpy/getting_started/troubleshooting/basics.md): Lots of stuff can go wrong during an analysis. The intent of this page is to guide you through navigating the inevitable errors associated with doing bioinformatics.
- [ Common Issues](https://pdimens.github.io/harpy/getting_started/troubleshooting/common_issues.md): If you use bamutils clipOverlap on alignments that are used for the or modules, they will cause both programs to error. We don't know why, but they do.
- [ harpy diagnose](https://pdimens.github.io/harpy/getting_started/troubleshooting/diagnose.md): Harpy includes a series of commands to faciliate troubleshooting
- [ Install / Conda](https://pdimens.github.io/harpy/getting_started/troubleshooting/conda.md): Conda is an awesome package manager, but was slow and used a ton of memory as dependencies increased. Recent (23.10+) versions of Conda now use the libmamba solver, the super-fast and super-lightweight solver from Mamba.
- [ Resume](https://pdimens.github.io/harpy/getting_started/troubleshooting/resume.md): When calling a workflow (e.g. ), Harpy performs various file checks and validations, sets up the Snakemake command, output folder(s), etc. In the event you want to continue a failed or manually terminated workflow without overwriting the workflow files (e.g.
- [ View](https://pdimens.github.io/harpy/getting_started/troubleshooting/view.md): This convenience command lets you view relevant details within a Harpy workflow directory without having to fish around for the right files. Since version 4.0, a harpy installation also aliases harpy view as hv for convenience.
- [ Common Harpy Options](https://pdimens.github.io/harpy/getting_started/common_options.md): Command-line options across harpy workflows
- [ Input FASTQ Format](https://pdimens.github.io/harpy/getting_started/inputformat.md): What FASTQ formats are compatible with Harpy
- [ Install Harpy](https://pdimens.github.io/harpy/getting_started/install.md): Installing Harpy on your system
- [ Intro to linked-read data](https://pdimens.github.io/harpy/getting_started/linked_read_data.md): What you ought to know about linked reads
- [ Harpy Reports](https://pdimens.github.io/harpy/getting_started/reports.md): The report system in Harpy

## Workflows

### Align

- [ Align Sequences to a Genome](https://pdimens.github.io/harpy/workflows/align/align.md): Choosing an aligner
- [ Map Reads to a reference with BWA MEM](https://pdimens.github.io/harpy/workflows/align/bwa.md): Align haplotagged sequences with BWA-MEM2
- [ Map Reads to a reference with strobealign](https://pdimens.github.io/harpy/workflows/align/strobe.md): Align haplotagged sequences with strobealign
- [ Create a Genome Assembly](https://pdimens.github.io/harpy/workflows/assembly.md): Create a genome assembly from linked reads
- [ Resolve barcodes shared by different molecules](https://pdimens.github.io/harpy/workflows/deconvolve.md): Resolve barcodes shared by different molecules
- [ Preprocess Raw Sequences](https://pdimens.github.io/harpy/workflows/preprocess.md): Preprocess raw sequences into haplotag barcoded samples
- [ Impute Genotypes using Sequences](https://pdimens.github.io/harpy/workflows/impute.md): Impute genotypes for linked-read data with STITCH
- [ Create a Metagenome Assembly](https://pdimens.github.io/harpy/workflows/metassembly.md): Create a metagenome assembly from linked reads
- [Template](https://pdimens.github.io/harpy/workflows/template.md): Generate extra files for analysis with Harpy

### Phase

- [ Phase Alignments from Haplotypes](https://pdimens.github.io/harpy/workflows/phase/phase_alignment.md): Phasing will add PS tags to alignment records using Whatshap
- [ Phase SNPs into Haplotypes](https://pdimens.github.io/harpy/workflows/phase/phase_snp.md): Phase SNPs and indels into haplotypes with HapCut2
- [ Validation checks for input files](https://pdimens.github.io/harpy/workflows/validate.md): Perform file format checks on linked-read FASTQ/BAM files
- [ Quality Trim Sequences](https://pdimens.github.io/harpy/workflows/qc.md): Quality trim haplotagged sequences with fastp
- [ Call SNPs and small indels](https://pdimens.github.io/harpy/workflows/snp.md): Call SNPs and small indels

### SV

- [ Find structural variants](https://pdimens.github.io/harpy/workflows/sv/sv.md): Find structural variants
- [ Call Structural Variants using LEVIATHAN](https://pdimens.github.io/harpy/workflows/sv/leviathan.md): Call structural variants using Leviathan
- [ Call Structural Variants using NAIBR](https://pdimens.github.io/harpy/workflows/sv/naibr.md): Call structural variants using NAIBR (plus)
